title: Deploy Stored Procedure Components
text: Deploy Stored Procedure Components Edit the $SSP path in the appropriate assignment file to point to the location of your stored procedure components. You must explicitly map the $SSP path, because it has no default mapping. If a component has multiple implementations (including a stored procedure component which is not set to default), use the [SERVICES_EXEC] section to explicitly assign the component to a DBMS connection. Review transaction control within your application. By default, DBMS transaction control is not affected by the execution of a stored procedure. However, if transaction control is manipulated within a stored procedure, Uniface cannot guarantee a correct transaction state. Note: Multiple (parallel) transactions are supported. A stored procedure runs in the same transaction as the calling Uniface component. Example: Mapping Stored Procedure Components to a Connection All stored procedure components are mapped to the $ORA2 (via $SSP) connection, with the exeption of component_1, which is mapped to the $ORA1 connection. [PATHS] $ORA1 =ORA:|scott|tiger $ORA2 =ORA:|system|manager $SSP =$ORA2 [SERVICES_EXEC] component_1 = $ORA1:component_1 Copy Related Links Naming Specifications for Service Store Procedures For Service Stored Procedures, the signature of the Stored Procedure Component maps the names of Uniface components, operations, and parameters to the name of a package, procedure, and parameter names in the procedure.
